summary: Gate exited 0 but Phase 1 acceptance/spec are not met: pytest did not run (gate masks failures), Langfuse is unreachable but treated as optional (and soak ignores 100% Langfuse failures), Deal API tooling is wired to /api/deals and transition_deal is probe-only/422 with no approval fail-closed, checkpoint encryption is not applied to persisted checkpoints (plaintext DB writes; no SecurePostgresSaver), and the PII canary gate is simulated and skips DB/traces. These are recurring issues from prior cycles (potential stuck loop).

blockers.title: Phase1 gate is not fail-closed; pytest did not run (missing `pipefail` masks failures)
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` runs pytest through a pipeline (`python3 -m pytest ... | tee ... | tail ...`) without `set -o pipefail`, so the pipeline exit status is `tail`, not pytest. The pytest gate is also marked optional (`required=false`). Evidence: `gate_artifacts/phase1/pytest_output.log` contains `/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest`, yet the gate reported `pytest: PASSED` and exited 0. This violates acceptance criteria: `pytest tests/` must pass and the gate must exit nonzero on any failure.
blockers.expected: Gate fails if pytest is missing or any test fails; `pytest tests/` actually runs and must pass.
blockers.actual: Gate reports `pytest: PASSED` and prints `PHASE1_GATE=PASSED` even when pytest is missing.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make the gate fail-closed: use `set -euo pipefail` (or check `PIPESTATUS`) and avoid pipelines that discard the primary command’s exit code; mark pytest as required; run pytest from a known project environment (e.g., `python -m pip install -e '.[dev]'` or `./venv/bin/python -m pytest`).
validation_steps: Intentionally break a test and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero.
Run the gate in an env without pytest and confirm it fails with a clear error.
Run the gate after installing deps and confirm pytest executes and passes.

blockers.title: Langfuse is unreachable but treated as optional; soak passes despite 100% Langfuse failures
blockers.details: Acceptance requires Langfuse reachable at :3001 and `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` returning 200. Current artifacts show Langfuse is unreachable (`gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_health.json: healthy=false`). However `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` runs Langfuse health as optional (`required=false`), and `scripts/gates/soak_24h.sh` explicitly treats Langfuse failures as non-critical, resulting in `gate_artifacts/phase1/soak_smoke.json` reporting `langfuse` failures = 60 while `verdict=PASS`.
blockers.expected: Gate fails if Langfuse is not reachable; soak fails (or at minimum marks FAIL) when Langfuse health fails for the entire run.
blockers.actual: Langfuse health is `unreachable` but phase1 gate exits 0 and soak reports PASS with 60/60 Langfuse failures.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make Langfuse mandatory for Phase 1: mark Langfuse health gate required, and treat Langfuse failures as failures in the soak harness; ensure Langfuse is started (e.g., via docker-compose) before gates run.
validation_steps: Start Langfuse and confirm `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` returns 200.
Stop Langfuse and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero.
Re-run soak and confirm Langfuse failures cause FAIL.

blockers.title: Deal API endpoints do not match decision-locked contract (`/deals` expected, `/api/deals` used)
blockers.details: Authoritative roadmap locks tool mapping to Deal API endpoints: `GET /deals`, `GET /deals/{id}`, `POST /deals/{id}/transition` (see `/home/zaks/bookkeeping/docs/ZakOps-Ultimate-Implementation-Roadmap-combine.md`). Current implementation and probes are wired to `/api/deals/*` (e.g., `src/core/agent/deal_api_client.py` uses `/api/deals`, and `scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py` sets required_endpoints to `/api/deals/*`). The probe output is also misleading: it prints `GET /deals` while calling `/api/deals`.
blockers.expected: All Deal API tool wiring and probes use the locked `/deals` endpoints, and contract probe reports the same paths it actually calls.
blockers.actual: Client/tests/probe are wired to `/api/deals/*`, while the authoritative contract specifies `/deals/*`; the probe prints `/deals` even when calling `/api/deals`.
blockers.recommended_fix: Align to the decision-locked contract: either (a) update the Deal API service to expose `/deals`, `/deals/{id}`, `/deals/{id}/transition` (and OpenAPI) or (b) if `/api/deals` is the true contract, update the authoritative docs/spec and acceptance criteria accordingly (requires human decision). Ensure the probe derives/prints endpoints consistently (ideally from OpenAPI) and fails if required endpoints are missing.
validation_steps: Re-run the contract probe and assert `required_endpoints.*.path` match the locked spec.
Run the phase1 gate with Deal API down and confirm it fails (no silent PASS).

blockers.title: CRITICAL `transition_deal` is not fail-closed on missing approval and does not successfully transition (schema mismatch → 422/probe-only)
blockers.details: `src/core/agent/deal_tools.py` proceeds to call the Deal API even when `approval_id` is missing (it only logs a warning). Additionally, the Deal API in this repo expects `TransitionRequest(to_stage, reason, approved_by)` at `POST /api/deals/{deal_id}/transition` (`src/api/deal_lifecycle/main.py`), but `src/core/agent/deal_api_client.py` sends `{new_stage, reason, actor_id}`. This mismatch is consistent with the gate artifact showing `http_status: 422` and `probe_mode: true` (no real transition). Phase 1 requires transition_deal to execute only after approval and to call the real Deal API successfully.
blockers.expected: Without approval context, `transition_deal_tool` returns `APPROVAL_REQUIRED` and MUST NOT contact the Deal API. With a valid approval context and correct payload schema, Deal API returns 2xx and the artifact shows a real (non-probe) transition.
blockers.actual: Tool can call Deal API without approval_id, and the transition call is schema-incompatible (observed 422). Gate artifacts prove only endpoint probing, not a real transition.
blockers.recommended_fix: Enforce approval in `transition_deal_tool` (fail closed before any HTTP). Update `DealAPIClient.transition_deal` to match the Deal API contract (field names and required fields, including `approved_by`). Update the gate to demonstrate a real approved transition (in a safe test environment) and assert 2xx status and stage change.
validation_steps: Call `transition_deal_tool` without approval and confirm no outbound HTTP occurs.
Call with an approved context and confirm the Deal API returns 2xx and the deal stage changes.
Confirm `gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_transition_deal.json` indicates non-probe execution and success=true.

blockers.title: Checkpoint encryption is not applied to persisted checkpoints; `SecurePostgresSaver` is missing and DB writes are plaintext
blockers.details: Phase 1 requires checkpoint persistence encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) and kill-9 resume working with encryption. While `src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py` implements an encryptor, it is not integrated anywhere in the codebase (no uses of `encrypt_checkpoint_data`). The actual checkpoint persistence layer `src/core/hitl/checkpoint.py` writes `checkpoint_data` as plaintext JSON (`json.dumps(checkpoint_data)`) into `zakops.execution_checkpoints`. Also, `SecurePostgresSaver` is referenced only in a docstring and does not exist. Current encryption/kill-9 gates only test in-memory encryption and simulated recovery, not DB-backed encrypted resume.
blockers.expected: Checkpoint data written to Postgres is ciphertext (e.g., `ENC:v1:` prefix) and decryption is applied on read; key is enforced in production mode; kill-9 test demonstrates real crash/restart resume from DB-backed encrypted checkpoint.
blockers.actual: CheckpointStore persists plaintext JSON; encryption helpers are unused; no SecurePostgresSaver exists; kill-9/encryption tests are simulated/in-memory.
blockers.recommended_fix: Implement encryption at the persistence boundary: either create `SecurePostgresSaver` per spec and wire it into runtime, or integrate `encrypt_checkpoint_data`/`decrypt_checkpoint_data` into `CheckpointStore` save/load paths. Add a DB-backed test that asserts ciphertext-at-rest (no plaintext) and a real kill-9 restart/resume test.
validation_steps: Create a checkpoint and verify the DB row contains ciphertext and no plaintext strings.
Run a real kill -9 + restart and confirm resume completes correctly using encrypted persisted state.
Run phase1 gate and confirm encryption failures stop the gate.

blockers.title: PII canary gate does not provide deterministic proof (simulated injection; DB/traces scans can be skipped)
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh` explicitly states canary injection is simulated (no real agent invocation). It also silently skips DB scanning when `psql` is unavailable and skips trace scanning when directories are missing, yet still returns PASS. The generated report lacks trace locations and even includes a spurious empty-string violation (`"violations":[""]`). This does not satisfy the stop-ship requirement to prove the canary does NOT appear in logs/traces/DB.
blockers.expected: Gate injects the canary through the real agent/tool path and deterministically scans logs, traces (Langfuse), and DB; it fails closed if any scan cannot run.
blockers.actual: Injection is simulated; DB/traces scans can be skipped; gate still returns PASS.
blockers.recommended_fix: Implement real canary injection (invoke agent with canary content) and deterministic scanning: query DB via asyncpg (no dependency on psql), export/search traces from Langfuse API, and scan logs. Fail closed if Langfuse/DB/log scans cannot be performed; fix the empty-string violations list bug.
validation_steps: Run canary gate and confirm it scans DB and Langfuse traces (not skipped).
Introduce a controlled canary leak and confirm the gate fails.
Confirm `pii_canary_report.json` shows `violations: []` on success.

majors.title: Agent API port and orchestration stack do not match Decision Lock (:8095 + LangGraph/PostgresSaver)
majors.details: Decision Lock quick reference locks Agent API to :8095 and orchestration to LangGraph + PostgresSaver. This repo’s local stack and gates target :8091 (e.g., `docker-compose.yml` maps backend to 8091 and `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` defaults BACKEND_URL to 8091). This is a spec mismatch and undermines Phase 1 portability.
majors.expected: Agent API binds to :8095 as locked; orchestration uses LangGraph + PostgresSaver per Decision Lock.
majors.actual: Local stack defaults to :8091 and there is no evidence of LangGraph + PostgresSaver integration.
majors.recommended_fix: Align ports and orchestration to Decision Lock: expose Agent API on :8095 (or add a dedicated agent-api service) and ensure LangGraph + PostgresSaver are the orchestration/checkpoint mechanism (or update the locked docs via human decision if the architecture has changed).
validation_steps: Confirm `curl http://localhost:8095/health/ready` succeeds after bring-up.
Update gates to default to :8095 and confirm they still pass when services are running.

minors.title: Deal API contract probe uses deprecated `datetime.utcnow()`
minors.details: Gate output shows a DeprecationWarning from `scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py` using `datetime.utcnow()`. This is noise and can be fixed with timezone-aware UTC timestamps.
minors.expected: No deprecation warnings; timestamps are timezone-aware UTC.
minors.recommended_fix: Replace `datetime.utcnow()` with `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` and format as ISO8601 UTC.
validation_steps: Re-run the probe and confirm no DeprecationWarning is emitted.

minors.title: Langfuse docker-compose includes default secret fallbacks
minors.details: `docker-compose.yml` provides default values for `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` and `SALT` via `${...:-...}`. Phase 1 security requirements emphasize env-based secrets and no default secrets in production mode; defaults increase risk of misconfiguration.
minors.expected: Secrets are required via env in production; dev defaults are gated or fail-closed when `ENVIRONMENT=production`.
minors.recommended_fix: Gate defaults behind development mode or require explicit env vars when `ENVIRONMENT` indicates production; document required Langfuse secret env vars.
validation_steps: Set `ENVIRONMENT=production` and confirm startup fails fast unless required secrets are provided.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Fix `phase1_gate.sh` (and any sub-gates) to be fail-closed: add `pipefail`, stop masking exit codes, and make pytest a required gate that actually runs.
how_to_validate: Break a test and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero.
Run without pytest installed and confirm the gate fails with a clear message.
Install deps and confirm pytest runs and passes.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Make Langfuse mandatory and verifiable: ensure :3001 is reachable, require the health check, and make soak fail when Langfuse is down.
how_to_validate: Start Langfuse and confirm `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` returns 200.
Stop Langfuse and confirm phase1 gate and soak report FAIL/nonzero.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Align Deal API endpoints to the decision-locked contract (or escalate for a human decision): update probe/client/tests to use the agreed paths and report them consistently.
how_to_validate: Run the contract probe and confirm the reported required endpoints match the locked spec.
Run the deal tools gate with Deal API stopped and confirm it fails (no silent PASS).

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Fix `transition_deal`: enforce approval_id fail-closed and match the Deal API transition request schema so a real approved transition succeeds (2xx).
how_to_validate: Call transition without approval and confirm no outbound HTTP occurs.
Call transition with approval and confirm Deal API returns 2xx and stage changes.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Encrypt checkpoint persistence at rest: wire encryption into the real checkpoint store/saver and add DB-backed ciphertext + real kill-9 resume verification.
how_to_validate: Persist a checkpoint and verify the DB row contains ciphertext (no plaintext).
Perform a real kill -9 + restart and confirm resume works using encrypted persisted state.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Replace the simulated PII canary gate with a real injection + deterministic scans of logs/traces/DB; fail closed when scans cannot run.
how_to_validate: Run the canary gate and confirm it scans DB and Langfuse traces (not skipped).
Introduce a controlled canary leak and confirm the gate fails.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Align ports to Decision Lock: ensure the Agent API is on :8095 (and gates default to it) and reconcile the orchestration/checkpoint stack with LangGraph + PostgresSaver.
how_to_validate: Confirm Agent API responds on :8095 and the phase1 gate defaults target it.
Confirm Decision Lock alignment is documented and verifiable.

evidence: gate_artifacts/phase1/pytest_output.log contains `/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest` while the phase1 gate reported `pytest: PASSED`.
scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:109 runs pytest through a pipeline without `pipefail` and marks it optional.
gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_health.json shows `{\"healthy\": false, ...}` and `gate_artifacts/phase1/soak_smoke.json` shows `failures_by_service.langfuse = 60` with `verdict = PASS`.
/home/zaks/bookkeeping/docs/ZakOps-Ultimate-Implementation-Roadmap-combine.md:125 locks Deal API tool mapping to `/deals/*`.
src/api/deal_lifecycle/main.py:92 defines `TransitionRequest` as `{to_stage, reason, approved_by}`, but src/core/agent/deal_api_client.py:434 sends `{new_stage, reason, actor_id}`.
src/core/hitl/checkpoint.py:204 persists `checkpoint_data` via `json.dumps(checkpoint_data)` (plaintext), and no code references `encrypt_checkpoint_data(...)`.
scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:125 states injection is simulated and skips DB checks if `psql` is missing.
